subtitle: Release Train Execution · Agile Metrics · Colorado-Based
hero_sub: I facilitate 2–3 concurrent teams, create the metrics that actually drive improvement, and remove impediments before they become problems. Georgetown, CO — 45 minutes from Denver.

the_case.need: Facilitate agile scrum team execution for 2–3 scrum teams
the_case.answer: Eptura: ~10 teams simultaneously. A&E: ~10 concurrent workstreams. Paperless Post: 2 teams. DigitasLBI: 2 teams. Supporting 2–3 is the comfortable range.
the_case.detail: Multi-team facilitation is the standard pattern of my career. I've managed up to 10 concurrent teams while maintaining individual coaching relationships with each PO and tech lead. 2–3 teams is a focused, effective zone.

the_case.need: Create, modify, publish and leverage standardized Agile Metrics
the_case.answer: Built standardized metrics at Eptura across ~10 teams: velocity, burndown, cycle time, team health. Published in ADO, Jira, and Smartsheet. Used metrics to identify trends and target improvement.
the_case.detail: Metrics are a coaching tool, not a reporting obligation. At Eptura I built the metrics infrastructure that gave leadership visibility and gave me the patterns to drive targeted coaching. The 35%+ efficiency gain and 20% carryover reduction came from acting on what the metrics showed — not just publishing them.

the_case.need: Release train execution and SAFe environment
the_case.answer: PI Planning and release train coordination at Eptura (enterprise SaaS) and DigitasLBI/SunTrust (SAFe, PI-aligned, SOX-compliant financial services).
the_case.detail: I've operated in PI-aligned release train environments at two distinct scales: Eptura (enterprise SaaS PMO, ~10 teams) and DigitasLBI (SAFe enterprise, regulated financial services). PI Planning, ART coordination, and release train dependencies are established practice.

the_case.need: Work with multiple in-house and external Scrum teams enterprise-wide
the_case.answer: Scrum-of-Scrums across ~10 teams at Eptura and ~6 teams at A&E. Cross-team dependency management and horizontal alignment at program level.
the_case.detail: Enterprise-wide coordination requires a different skillset than team-level facilitation. I've facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums, managed cross-team dependencies, and maintained horizontal alignment across programs with up to 10 teams — with onshore and offshore components.

the_case.need: Provide coaching on agile practices for assigned and other scrum teams
the_case.answer: Agile coaching across all 10 teams at Eptura. NEXUS Scrum coaching at Paperless Post. Agile adoption from scratch at Underline and in the Marketing department at Eptura.
the_case.detail: Coaching scales when it's structured. I built Agile training packages at Paperless Post, facilitated org-wide mentorship at Eptura, and coached teams through varying maturity levels with tailored approaches. Not one-size-fits-all.

the_case.answer: Direct ADO experience at Understood.org (2019–2020), Eptura (2022–2023), and Crocs (2026). Board configuration, workflow management, sprint tracking, delivery dashboards.
the_case.detail: ADO is my primary enterprise delivery tool alongside Jira. At Eptura I was the ADO configuration owner for the PMO — standardizing workflows, fields, and dashboards across all teams. At Crocs I used ADO for release readiness tracking and go/no-go reporting.

the_stack.headline: The delivery toolset.
the_stack.subhead: The tools I use to run clean delivery and surface what matters.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Enterprise delivery tracking — boards, sprint tracking, release dashboards, dependency management
nodes.pm_role: Configure and maintain board structure, standardize workflows across teams, build delivery health dashboards, and track release train dependencies.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Team-level delivery tracking — epics, stories, sprint boards, burndown, velocity
nodes.pm_role: Sprint board management, backlog grooming support, metric tracking. Jira AI used for backlog synthesis and sprint summaries.

nodes.label: Smartsheet
nodes.desc: Program-level reporting, roadmap tracking, and resource capacity planning
nodes.pm_role: Leadership dashboards, milestone tracking, cross-team dependency mapping, and capacity planning against sprint commitments.

nodes.label: Microsoft Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted reporting, stakeholder communication, and administrative overhead reduction
nodes.pm_role: Reduces time on status reporting and summary documentation — freeing capacity for actual coaching and impediment removal.

nodes.label: Confluence
nodes.desc: Documentation, team agreements, and process frameworks
nodes.pm_role: Working agreements, Definition of Done, retrospective-to-action tracking, and process improvement documentation.

when_things_break.scenario: Sprint carryover rate is increasing across two consecutive sprints
response: Diagnose before acting: capacity problem, readiness problem, or commitment problem?
Pull burndown data and story completion rates — find the pattern, not just the symptom
Partner with PO on backlog readiness — are stories entering sprint execution-ready?
Introduce WIP limits if the team is context-switching between too many items
Retrospective: what specifically changed between the last clean sprint and now?

when_things_break.scenario: Cross-team dependency blocking two teams simultaneously mid-sprint
response: Convene an immediate Scrum-of-Scrums — not at the next scheduled cadence
Map the dependency chain: who owns the blocker, what unblocks it, how long?
Assess sprint goal impact: can impacted work be swapped without breaking commitments?
Escalate to leadership with a clear ask — decision needed, not just awareness
Update release train risk register; surface in next ART sync with resolution status

when_things_break.scenario: Release train metrics show velocity increasing but quality is declining
response: Velocity without quality is a vanity metric — name that directly to the team
Pull defect rate, escaped defects, and post-sprint bug counts alongside velocity
Investigate whether Definition of Done is actually being enforced or bypassed
Introduce quality gates at story level, not just sprint level
Retrospective with explicit quality framing: 'We're shipping more but breaking more — what's the trade-off we're making?'

how_i_run_a_room: Sprint ceremonies end with documented decisions and committed action items — not follow-up emails
Standardized metrics published before leadership asks for them
Impediments have owners and resolution timelines within 24 hours of surfacing
Backlog refinement produces execution-ready stories — no ambiguity entering sprint planning
Cross-team dependencies mapped and surfaced at Scrum-of-Scrums before they become blockers
Release train coordination runs on cadence — PI Planning, ART syncs, and system demos on schedule
Retrospective actions tracked sprint-over-sprint until resolved — not forgotten

points: Release train execution for a global e-commerce retailer — go/no-go governance across Engineering, QA, Change, and Business
ADO and Jira boards maintained throughout; delivery metrics published to leadership in real time
On-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization
PI-aligned delivery across ~10 distributed teams (India and California)
Created and published standardized Agile metrics in ADO, Jira, and Smartsheet
35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction
Mentored junior Scrum Masters; facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums enterprise-wide
Scrum Master for 2 teams in a SAFe, PI-aligned release train
SOX-compliant financial-services environment — governance-aligned transparency required
Tracked velocity and utilization via HP Agile Tracker and Rally
Coordinated offshore QA (Costa Rica) across time-zone delivery complexity
~10 concurrent SVOD workstreams across Web, iOS, tvOS, Android, Roku
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scroms across ~6 teams; managed cross-team dependencies
Published velocity and burndown metrics to leadership throughout
Shipped all platforms against externally visible launch windows

before: Metrics exist but don't drive any actual change in team behavior
Release train dependencies are discovered late — after they've already created problems
Sprint carryover is accepted as normal rather than diagnosed and fixed
You're doing impediment removal because the SM isn't catching things early enough
after: Metrics are published proactively and used to target specific coaching interventions
Dependencies are mapped and surfaced at Scrum-of-Scrums before they block delivery
Carryover is treated as a diagnostic signal, not a scheduling inconvenience — and it decreases
Impediments have owners and timelines within 24 hours — you never have to ask

the_ramp.deliverable: ADO and Jira access. Attend all active ceremonies as observer. Map current team dynamics, metrics baseline, and open impediments.
Facilitate first full ceremony cycle for 2–3 teams. Publish first standardized metrics report. Introduce one process improvement per retrospective.
Full ownership of all assigned team ceremonies. Cross-team dependency map complete. Release train coordination running on cadence.
Metrics infrastructure established and publishing regularly. First measurable improvement documented. No supervision needed — just clear escalation paths.
